questions.question: Your aircraft is currently loaded to 3647 LBS with a CG of 79.4". How much ballast do you need in the forward baggage compartment (12" aft) to bring the aircraft within the 78" aft CG limit?

A Boeing 747-400 (4-engine jet) burns 3,200 pounds of Jet-A per hour per operating engine and travels at 504 KTAS. Upon losing an engine, speed is reduced by 120 knots, and fuel consumption in the operating engines must increase by 20% to account for lost thrust. If an engine is lost 3.5 hours into your 7500 nm flight, how much will the actual landing weight differ from the planned landing weight?
Your aircraft is loaded to 2976 LBS with a CG of 62.9". You fly for 3 hours and 24 minutes, burn 17 gph and the fuel arm is 73". What is your landing CG?

You calculate your weight and balance prior to departure and come up with a gross weight of 2,435 LBS and CG of 18.9". Just prior to departure, Betty has an extra bag to get added. The new bag weighs 33 LBS and will be in the baggage compartment at 67". What is the new CG?

Congratulations! You have just passed your private pilot checkride! You are excited to take the FBO's trusty, if not slightly worn out, C182 for a few circuits around the airfield to exercise your PIC muscle. Your friend, Bonnie Propwash, comes along. After calculating the weight and balance, you realize you're going to need to add some ballast to the cargo area. The FBO has a bucket of ACA (American Cornhole Association) approved bags just for this purpose. They weigh 16 ounces each. What is the minimum number of cornhole bags you need to strap down in the cargo area to make the aircraft safe for flight? Here are the remaining details: [[TABLE]]

Your aircraft (3,350 LBS) is packed full, but unfortunately the CG (49") is too far aft of the limit of 48". How much weight do you have to remove from the baggage compartment (station 122) to be 1" forward of the limit?
Your current ramp weight is 2,437 LBS and your CG is 71.4". If the forward CG limit is 73", how many 5-KG ballast weights are required in the baggage compartment (94") to move the CG into the limit?
You add 36 gallons of Avgas into the fuel tank at an arm of 23". If the aircraft weighed 2,167 lbs and had a CG of 46.57" before refueling, what will the CG be after fueling?

Your aircraft weighs 1,048 lbs with a CG of 18.3" empty. You (173 lbs) and your instructor (184 lbs) are planning to do acrobatic training, but you notice that the fuel tanks are full at 28 gallons. If you burn 5.8 GPH, how long will you need to fly before the aircraft is within the aerobatic CG range of 16-18"? You and your instructor sit at 15" aft, and the fuel tank is at 24" aft.
You are above your maximum landing weight of 34,137 kg. You currently weigh 75,980 lbs. You are burning 3,300 lbs/hr in fuel. How many minutes will you need to wait before you can land?

Assume the standard weight of each passenger is 200 pounds. You receive the following zone count: [[TABLE]] The empty weight of the aircraft is 48,600 lbs, 17,000 lbs of gas and 2,000 lbs of bags are added. What is the gross takeoff weight?
Your landing weight and CG with 9 gallons of Avgas on board is 1,948 lbs and 73.1". If you top off the 45-gallon fuel tank (arm of 79.1"), what is the new weight and CG?

You're about to head off on a short ski trip with friends and have finished your weight and balance calculations. Your plane weighs 3,188 pounds and its CG is 44.12". Just before you get ready to start the engines, one of your friends realized he left his bag of clothes in the car. If he adds a 38 pound bag to station 122.2", what is your new CG?

Your aircraft weighs 2,060 lbs with a CG of 13.7". You want to load baggage, located at area 77", until you reach the aircraft's maximum gross weight of 2,500 lbs. What will your CG be after you are loaded to the aircraft's maximum gross weight?

Your aircraft's empty weight/CG is 1734 lbs/32.7". You weigh 177 lbs and sit in the pilot's seat at an arm of 31". With 22 gallons of fuel at 39" and 10 lbs of baggage at 68", what is your ramp weight/CG?
Your aircraft weighs 1097 kgs empty. If you put 128 liters of fuel in the tanks, what percentage of the total weight is comprised of fuel?

Your aircraft weighs 1,437 lbs empty with a CG of 39.7". You weigh 195 lbs and will sit in the pilot's seat at 33", and you have 27 gallons of fuel in the tanks at 44". How many quarts of oil can you carry in the baggage compartment (130") without exceeding the aft CG limit of 42"?

You are planning to fly a couple friends on a weekend trip and have calculated your weight at 2175 LBS and CG of 15.7". At the last minute another friend decides to join. They weigh 145 LBS and will sit in the rear seat 55". What is the new CG?
During preflight, you intend to add some oil to your engine. You add 63% of a gallon. How many pounds did you add?
The standard weight and balance for you with full fuel is 1730 LBS and CG of 26.3". If you bring two friends (150 LBS and 210 LBS) in the back seat at 51" what will be your new CG?
Reference the previous question. During the flight you burn off 26 gallons from 21". What will your CG be upon landing?
questions.context: Referenced question 32: The standard weight and balance for you with full fuel is 1730 LBS and CG of 26.3". If you bring two friends (150 LBS and 210 LBS) in the back seat at 51" what will be your new CG?

questions.question: You have decided to upgrade your old aluminum prop to a new composite one! The old prop weighed 62 LBS while the composite model weights 41 LBS. Prior to the swap your aircraft weighed 1475 LBS with a CG of 11.63". If the prop is at -61" what will the new CG fall after the swap?

With you, a passenger, and full fuel, your aircraft weighs in at 902 kilograms with a CG of 43.7". How much baggage can you carry in your cargo compartment (73.2" aft) without exceeding the CG limit of 45"?

You depart with full fuel (46 gallons), and fly a 448 NM flight at 127 knots ground speed. If your aircraft burns 9.6 GPH and the fuel arm is 45.9", what is your landing CG? You took off at 2,180 LBS with a CG of 32.4".
Your fully loaded aircraft weighs 2,160 LBS with a CG of 48". If you then load 35 US gallons of fuel at an average station of 36" what will the resultant aircraft CG be?

You have completed your planned weight and balance and determined your aircraft should weigh 2,570 LBS with a CG of 43.6". When one of your passengers arrives they inform you their dog has to come on the trip. Fine, but it's going in the back! What will be your new CG if it only weighs 15 LBS and will be located at 87"?
Your fully loaded aircraft weighs 2,160 lbs with a CG of 48". If you then load 35 US gallons of fuel at an average station of 36", what will the resultant aircraft CG be?
Your aircraft is loaded to 1016 kg with a CG of 32.4. What is your takeoff CG after the aircraft is serviced with 73 liters of 100LL at a station of 48"?

Another student wishes to observe your training flight, so you agree to let them ride in the back. Your instructor asks you to calculate the weight and balance to make sure everything is within limits for the flight. If the weight and CG before the other student gets in is 2,018 pounds and 41.2 inches, what is the new weight and CG if the student weighs 190 pounds and sits at station 73?
Your aircraft is loaded to 2,480 lbs with a CG of 76.8". What will your new CG be if your 180 lb friend decides to join in the back seat at 108"?
In the previous question if your aircraft weighed 3410 LBS and had a CG of 57.3" before departure what will be the CG at your destination assuming the fuel was burned from 64.8"?
questions.context: Referenced question 20: How much avgas was burned after an aircraft flew 385 kilometers at 177 miles per hour? The average rate of burn was 0.88 liters per minute.

questions.question: You desire to transport AVGAS for personal use in a home-made trailer from the municipal airport to the grass runway you have created on your land. Your understanding of the regulations is that the trailer must not be designed to carry more than 450 liters of fuel, but you know that your tow vehicle can only safely pull 1000 pounds. How much can the empty trailer assembly weigh if you would like to carry the maximum regulatory limit and still be under 1000 pounds of gross weight?

Using the information from the previous question, upon fabricating your trailer, you find that its empty weight is 524 pounds. Which of these answers best describes how much fuel you can load it with and still be within the 1000-pound gross weight limit?

choices.text: less than half of the tank's capacity
two thirds of the tank's capacity

questions.correctChoice: two thirds of the tank's capacity
questions.context: Referenced question 31: You desire to transport AVGAS for personal use in a home-made trailer from the municipal airport to the grass runway you have created on your land. Your understanding of the regulations is that the trailer must not be designed to carry more than 450 liters of fuel, but you know that your tow vehicle can only safely pull 1000 pounds. How much can the empty trailer assembly weigh if you would like to carry the maximum regulatory limit and still be under 1000 pounds of gross weight?

questions.question: Your CG is aft of the limit by 1" at 78". How many pounds of ballast must you add at station 50, in order to bring the CG forward of the limit by 1"? Your aircraft currently weighs 1,124 KG.
Your aircraft weighs 5,560 lbs with a CG of 52.7". If your friend who weighs 175 lbs climbs into the backseat at a station of 72", what will the new CG be?

You are flying 6 skydivers each weighing 185 LBS in a Cessna 208. Your total weight and CG before they jump is 6085 LBS and 192.9". Half of them were seated at station 230" and the other half at station 270". What is your new CG after they jump?
You have your aircraft all loaded (4,630 LBS, CG of 76.3") when your friend shows up with a 35 LBS box of fish. If you put it in the aft baggage (station 150) what will be your new CG?

The max landing weight of your aircraft is 72,310 LBS. The Basic operating weight is 51,290 LBS. You must depart with at least 15,180 LBS of fuel to be legal and plan on burning 11,980 LBS of fuel on your flight. How much weight can you carry in payload and extra fuel without exceeding the max landing weight?

Pete and Repeat were in a flying boat and Pete jumped out. The flying boat was loaded per the table below: [[TABLE]] Takeoff CG: 141.08 Wt.: 4137.5 [[NEXT_LINE]] Landing CG: 141.38 Wt.: 3943.1 [[NEXT_LINE]] If both Pete and Repeat were seated in the forward seating area and weigh the same amount, what is the new landing Weight and CG?

The empty weight of a 55-liter fuel drum is 32 pounds. Your aircraft is capable of transporting 3250 pounds. Your customer prefers to ship full drums of heating oil that weighs 0.905 kilograms per liter, but will ship a single partial drum to comply with your limits. How many full drums can you carry to still be in the aircraft's capability?

As everyone is loading up in your airplane another friend decides they want to come along. You had planned to have a gross weight of 2436 LBS and a CG of 63.2". Your friend weighs 182 LBS and will sit in the back at station 116. What is your new CG?

It is a beautiful afternoon and you decide you are going to fly from Cherokee, Iowa to Des Moines, Iowa (TC = 132°) to pick up your three cousins and fly them around to see some farmland. Billy Jo weighs 45.3 kilograms, Sally Sue weighs 54.4 kilograms, and Cletus weighs 81.8 kilograms. For weight and balance purposes, how many pounds combined do your three cousins weigh?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 25-28.

questions.question: Regardless, you load the drum of AVGAS anyways, at an average station of 128". The CG before the AVGAS was loaded was 97". The new weight of the aircraft is 2950 lbs. What is the new CG?

You are ready to fly your aircraft with a weight of 3,650 lbs and a moment of 155,125 in- lbs, when you decide to fill your tip tanks with 100LL which hold 15 gallons per side at station 48. What will your new CG be?

A cargo plane is loaded on the ramp while the pilot calculates the weight and balance. The plane's ramp weight is 7,379 pounds at a CG of 193.2" when all of a sudden a last minute delivery truck drives up with 40 more boxes. Each box weighs 60 pounds. The boxes are put in Cargo Zone 2 (Station 141). What is the final ramp weight and CG once the boxes are loaded?
During the flight you burn 27 gallons of fuel from station 59 what will your CG be upon landing?
questions.context: Referenced question 34: You have the plane loaded to a gross weight of 4,260 lbs when your friends decide they want to change places. If Hank (190 lbs) moves to the back seat at station 73 and George (148 lbs) joins you up front at station 45 what will be your new CG assuming it was at 76.38"?

questions.question: How much AVGAS (station 79) has an airplane burned off that had a gross takeoff weight of 4,784 lbs. if the CG was 75.1" on takeoff and 74.8" on landing?

You recently departed in an aircraft that is fully loaded at 6290 lbs for takeoff. After flying for 45 minutes burning fuel at a rate of 35 gph, you find yourself in an unusual attitude and observe the aircraft in a 75° bank. How much does the aircraft weigh?
How much does the front passenger weigh?
questions.context: Use the following loading information to answer questions 25 through 29: [[TABLE]]

questions.question: How much fuel do you calculate is on board?
questions.context: Use the following loading information to answer questions 25 through 29: [[TABLE]]

questions.question: You find yourself ahead of the 63.5" forward CG limit. How many pounds of bags will you have to remove from the nose baggage to bring us within limits?
questions.context: Use the following loading information to answer questions 25 through 29: [[TABLE]]

questions.question: If a Cessna 172 is loaded up to 2,550 lbs, and your boyfriend, who weighs "175 pounds on the button" hops out of the back seat at station 73 inches, what's the new CG in inches? Original CG 105.918 cm.

You enter oceanic airspace over KEEKA at 1553 zulu. ATC assigns you a cruise speed of M .78 at FL370 burning 6,000 pounds of fuel per hour. ATC asks at what time you will be able to climb and maintain FL380. You look in the FMC and see that you weigh 161,000 pounds and that FL 370 is your maximum altitude currently. You research and notice that you can only weigh 150,000 pounds to reach FL 380. What time do you tell ATC?
Current CG = 34.7". Aft limit is 33.0". At 3700 lbs, what station would you have to delete 130 lbs from in order to shift the CG forward to the aft limit?
What is the landing weight and CG of an aircraft after it has flown for 7 hours burning 9.5 gallons per hour from station 89? It departed at 3370 lbs and a CG of 77.3".

Your loaded aircraft weighs 2900 pounds with a CG of 75 inches. Your maximum takeoff weight is 2800 pounds and the aft limit of the CG envelope is 73 inches. You decide to remove a portion of the 250 pounds of cargo at a station of 100 inches to bring the aircraft back into the allowable range. How much will you need to remove?
CG = 84.8" Gross weight = 8052 kg CG aft limit = 84.2" How much weight do we need to remove from station 123 in order to bring the aircraft within CG?
Aircraft Weight 6000 lbs CG location 79.4 What is the location of the CG if 78 lbs were removed from station 135?
Current aircraft weight 2945 lbs Current CG located at 76.7 What is the new location of the CG if 197 lbs were removed from station 119?

You're 42 lbs over gross weight with a CG of 103.4". To bring the aircraft within limits, we decide to remove the weight from station 155 which moves the CG forward.6". What is the new aircraft weight?

You and a couple of your friends are going to fly to Minneapolis to attend the Republican National Convention. You, John, and Sarah are all loaded up when Sarah realizes she left a bag in the trunk of her car. Assuming her 50 lb. bag is tossed into the cargo compartment (Station 141.5., what will the new CG be if the airplane's gross weight before the bag was added was 3,740 lbs with a CG of 118.0"?
Your aircraft is weighs 24,000 lbs and the CG is at station 120. If 250 kg are removed from station 179, how far forward does the CG move and what is the new CG?

choices.text: The CG moves 1.38 station units aft to station 121.38
The CG moves 1.38 station units forward to station 118.62
The CG moves 0.62 station units aft to station 120.62
The CG moves 0.62 station units forward to station 119.38

questions.correctChoice: The CG moves 1.38 station units forward to station 118.62

questions.question: A turboprop is loaded to a weight of 35,000 lbs. There is a system that allows for credits or reductions of weight for children onboard. The reduction is 100 lbs per child, and there are five children onboard (three at station 200 and two at station 400). What is the new CG if the original CG is at station 360?

You are flying a large aircraft weighing 125,000 kg. A pallet of cargo (1300 lbs) is removed from station 250. If this results in a aft movement of the CG of 2.2 inches, what is the original and new CG?

choices.text: Original CG at station 203.5 and new CG at station 205.7
Original CG at station 465 and new CG at station 467.2
Original CG at station 715 and new CG at station 717.2
Original CG at station 296.5 and new CG at station 298.7

questions.correctChoice: Original CG at station 715 and new CG at station 717.2

questions.question: When filled up, your Mooney's projected weight will be 2830 pounds, 130 pounds over its max. ramp weight. Your current CG is 71.00 inches with a forward limit of 70.2 inches. Where will you remove 130 lbs from the aircraft to move the CG to the forward limit?
Use the information from #17 for this calculation. If you burn 30 U.S. Gallons of AVGAS on this flight, what is your CG on touchdown?
questions.context: Tim has an aircraft with a basic empty weight of 1900 pounds (CG of 70 inches). Tim (180 pounds) has three friends he wants to take flying with him: Tiffany (120 pounds), John (270 pounds), and Genie (130 pounds). The fuel on board is 40 U.S. gallons of AVGAS and the baggage weighs 160 pounds. [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 17: If your buddy John switches places with Tiffany, what happens to the CG?

questions.question: John and Genie get out of the airplane to visit friends. Tim fills up the tanks (40 U.S. Gallons of AVGAS) and Tiffany returns to the front seat. What is the departure CG?
questions.context: Tim has an aircraft with a basic empty weight of 1900 pounds (CG of 70 inches). Tim (180 pounds) has three friends he wants to take flying with him: Tiffany (120 pounds), John (270 pounds), and Genie (130 pounds). The fuel on board is 40 U.S. gallons of AVGAS and the baggage weighs 160 pounds.

questions.question: Jim is going to operate an aircraft that weighs 2450 pounds with a CG of 87.4 inches. If Jim adds 140 kg of bags to station 120, what is the new CG?

Jeff wants to take Jane, who says, "I weigh the equivalent of 19 US gallons of Avgas" and her luggage (20 kg), Jill (125 lbs) and her luggage (20 lbs), and Jennifer (57 kg) with no luggage to Vegas. Jeff's aircraft, with him, the aircraft, and fuel, weighs 970 kg. If the max ramp and takeoff weight is 2560 lbs, can you take every one?
How much extra fuel can Jeff take to attain the new max ramp weight?
questions.context: Referenced question 7: Jeff wants to take Jane, who says, "I weigh the equivalent of 19 US gallons of Avgas" and her luggage (20 kg), Jill (125 lbs) and her luggage (20 lbs), and Jennifer (57 kg) with no luggage to Vegas. Jeff's aircraft, with him, the aircraft, and fuel, weighs 970 kg. If the max ramp and takeoff weight is 2560 lbs, can you take every one? [[DIVIDER]] Using information from question 7. Jeff switches to a new aircraft. The load and the aircraft weights are the same. However, the max ramp weight is 2650 lbs.

questions.question: Your current CG is 193.5 cm. The forward CG limit is 195 cm. If your aircraft weighs 1409 kg, how much weight will you need to remove from station 230 cm in order to you within CG?
Your aircraft currently weighs 2140 kg and your CG is 92.1". If you add 22 kg to station 74" what will be your new CG?
You're 2.3 inches forward of your forward CG limit of 83 inches. You decide to remove 34 lbs from station 59 in order to bring you within CG. What is your new weight?
If you were to add 90 lbs at station 120 to an aircraft that weighs 1864 kilograms (with a current CG of 83.6 inches), what is the new CG?
An aircraft weighs 1150 kilograms and 14 US gallons of AVGAS are added at station 41. If the original CG is 77.9 inches, what is the new CG?
